Brief.RawText:
* @brief Enumeration types define the enumerator names and values of Enumerations with a name matching
 * that of the Enumeration type.

Lines.FullText: The enumerator values are represented as a list of Integer objects. These values can be explicitly specified

Elements.RawText: The enumerator values are represented as a list of Integer objects. These values can be explicitly specified

Lines.FullText: during Enumeration type creation, or only the first enumerator value can be specified, with the rest

Elements.RawText: during Enumeration type creation, or only the first enumerator value can be specified, with the rest

Lines.FullText: automatically assigned in ascending order, starting from that value (or from the default '0' value if not

Elements.RawText: automatically assigned in ascending order, starting from that value (or from the default '0' value if not

Lines.FullText: specified).

Elements.RawText: specified).

Tags.RawText:
*
 * The enumerator values are represented as a list of Integer objects. These values can be explicitly specified
 * during Enumeration type creation, or only the first enumerator value can be specified, with the rest
 * automatically assigned in ascending order, starting from that value (or from the default '0' value if not
 * specified).

Lines.FullText: An Enumeration type provides a String-type name, a list of enumerator names (list of Strings) and

Elements.RawText: An Enumeration type provides a String-type name, a list of enumerator names (list of Strings) and

Lines.FullText: a list of enumerator values (list of Integer objects). To use Enumeration types for creating Enumeration

Elements.RawText: a list of enumerator values (list of Integer objects). To use Enumeration types for creating Enumeration

Lines.FullText: objects, they must be added to the Type Manager. Alternatively, if an Enumeration is created without

Elements.RawText: objects, they must be added to the Type Manager. Alternatively, if an Enumeration is created without

Lines.FullText: a matching Enumeration type in the manager, a default Enumeration type is generated based on the

Elements.RawText: a matching Enumeration type in the manager, a default Enumeration type is generated based on the

Lines.FullText: enumerator names and values of the created Enumeration object. This generated Enumeration type is then

Elements.RawText: enumerator names and values of the created Enumeration object. This generated Enumeration type is then

Lines.FullText: added to the Type Manager.

Elements.RawText: added to the Type Manager.

Description.RawText:
*
 * An Enumeration type provides a String-type name, a list of enumerator names (list of Strings) and
 * a list of enumerator values (list of Integer objects). To use Enumeration types for creating Enumeration
 * objects, they must be added to the Type Manager. Alternatively, if an Enumeration is created without
 * a matching Enumeration type in the manager, a default Enumeration type is generated based on the
 * enumerator names and values of the created Enumeration object. This generated Enumeration type is then
 * added to the Type Manager.
